items.title: What would a shift to the right mean for Chilean society? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>On Sunday, Chileans will head to the ballot box to elect a new president. For the first time since 2012, voting will be mandatory; those who do not vote will be fined. At a time when concerns over immigration and crime are at the forefront of voters' minds, Chile's governing leftist coalition is up against an array of right-wing challengers.

Chapters:
0:00 Voters in Chile to elect new president
3:37 Jacinta Molina, DW Correspondent

items.title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery near Moscow following drone attacks | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine says it has struck a Russian oil refinery near Moscow in retaliation for a wave of drone attacks on Kyiv and other cities. At least seven people were killed in the Ukrainian capital when Russian drones targeted power and water infrastructure, as well as residential areas. The UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres has condemned the attacks as violations of  international law. 

Chapters:
0:00 Ukraine says it has struck Russia
2:59 Mark Cancian, Center for Strategic & International Studies

items.title: What Trump would risk by putting US troops on Venezuelan soil | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump has signalled that the US is likely to scale up military action against Venezuela targeting alleged drug smuggling. A US task force in the Caribbean is expanding rapidly, and has carried out 20 strikes on suspected drug trafficking boats in the region. Donald Trump has threatened to expand these attacks onto Venezuelan soil, accusing President Nicolas Maduro of running a drug cartel. On board Air Force One, reporters pressed Trump on his plans.

Chapters:
0:00 Trump signals more action against Venezuela
0:52 John Feeley, Former US Ambassador to Panama

items.title: Massive attack of drones and missiles on Kyiv: What's Russia's strategy? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Six people have been killed in what Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has described as a "calculated" Russian attack on Kyiv. Dozens more were injured when the barrage of drones and missiles overwhelmed Ukraine's air defence systems in the early hours of Friday morning, targeting power and water infrastructure as well as residential areas. 

00:00 Kyiv hit by barrage of Russian drones and missiles

items.title: Deadly explosion rips through Kashmir police station | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Indian authorities say at least nine people have been killed and more than 30 injured in a blast at a police station. They say the detonation happened in the city of Srinagar in Indian-administered Kashmir. Police and forensic team members were handling confiscated explosives when the material accidentally blew up. It came just days after 13 people were killed in a blast in New Delhi. DW's New Delhi Bureau Chief Sandra Petersmann reports.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Returning to Syria: Between loss and hope | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Since the toppling of President Bashar al-Assad in December 2024, Syrian refugees and the internally displaced are gradually returning to their homes ‚Äď and encountering huge challenges in the process. Returning is difficult as many homes have been destroyed, the infrastructure is ailing and the nation‚Äôs recovery is being hampered by a severe economic crisis. Last but not least, the country‚Äôs political outlook is uncertain. We meet three individuals who have nevertheless taken the courageous decision to go back home:
Fatima Zahraa‚ÄĮcame back to Aleppo to raise her children in a familiar culture, while Amr‚ÄĮYakan gave up a secure life in Canada to rebuild his ancestors‚Äô mosque. After losing his family in the 2023 earthquake, filmmaker Fadi Al-Khatib returned to Damascus in 2023, determined to continue realizing his brother‚Äôs dream. Despite the destruction and deprivation, these Syrians choose hope over exile and are rebuilding not only houses, but also their identity. Their stories illustrate the emotional complexity involved in returning to a country still etched by conflict. A reportage by Mohamad Chreyteh.

Chapters: 
00:00 Intro 
01:10 Fatima returns from Germany   
03:37 A new hope for Aleppo?   
04:53 Amr feels a sense of duty 
07:57 Devastation in Damascus 
08:30 A new start for Fadi in Damascus

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Is Trump‚Äôs new Epstein ties investigation ‚Äėan act of hope‚Äô or ‚Äėdesperation‚Äô? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump says he has asked the Justice Department to investigate convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein‚Äôs ties to many other high-profile figures.  In a post on Truth Social, the US President said he would ask Attorney General Pam Bondi and the FBI to look into Epstein's links to top Democrats -- including former President Bill Clinton. His request follows the release by Congress of emails raising new questions about President Trump's ties to the convicted sex offender. Bondi has confirmed she will launch the investigation on Trump‚Äôs orders. 

00:00 Trump asks Justice Department to investigate Epstein ties with other high-profile people. 
01:04  Michael A. Genovese, Professor of Political Science at Loyola Marymount University in Los Angeles. 
05:34 Timothy Hagle is Associate Professor of Political Science at the University of Iowa.

items.title: Is Russia stockpiling weapons to overwhelm Ukraine‚Äôs defense at one go? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>On Thursday night-Friday morning, Russia launched a massive overnight attack on Kyiv, using hundreds of drones and at least eighteen missiles. Multiple residential buildings across the Ukrainian capital were hit, with emergency services reporting multiple deaths and injuries. Ukrainian authorities say this is the biggest strike on Kyiv in almost three weeks, and repeated their calls to allies to supply more air-defence systems. 

00:00 Russia strikes Kyiv in large-scale overnight attack
03:11 Military analyst Marina Miron from King's College London. 
11:05 Domitilla Sagramoso, Senior Lecturer in Security and Development at King's College London. 
15:08 DW correspondent Nick Connolly
